# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

# Elasticsearch Snapshot Configuration

# 1. docker-compose.yml - Infrastructure setup
# File: docker-compose.yml

version: '3.8'

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0
    container_name: es-backup-node
    environment:
      - node.name=es-backup-node
      - cluster.name=backup-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.keystore.path=/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/http.p12
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.keystore.path=/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/transport.p12
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/elastic_password
      - path.repo=/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms2g -Xmx2g"
      - xpack.security.audit.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.include=access_denied,authentication_failed,connection_denied,tampered_request
    secrets:
      - elastic_password
      - s3_access_key
      - s3_secret_key
    volumes:
      - es-data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - es-backups:/usr/share/elasticsearch/backups
      - es-certs:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs:ro
      - ./config/elasticsearch.yml:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:ro
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9200:9200"
    networks:
      - es-backup-net
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 4g
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -sf --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health || exit 1"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
    read_only: true
    tmpfs:
      - /tmp

secrets:
  elastic_password:
    file: ./secrets/elastic_password.txt
  s3_access_key:
    file: ./secrets/s3_access_key.txt
  s3_secret_key:
    file: ./secrets/s3_secret_key.txt

volumes:
  es-data:
    driver: local
  es-backups:
    driver: local
  es-certs:
    driver: local

networks:
  es-backup-net:
    driver: bridge
    internal: false

---
# 2. Elasticsearch configuration
# File: config/elasticsearch.yml

cluster.name: backup-cluster
node.name: es-backup-node

path.data: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
path.logs: /usr/share/elasticsearch/logs
path.repo:
  - /usr/share/elasticsearch/backups

network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200

discovery.type: single-node

xpack.security.enabled: true
xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: true

xpack.security.http.ssl:
  enabled: true
  keystore.path: certs/http.p12

xpack.security.transport.ssl:
  enabled: true
  verification_mode: certificate
  keystore.path: certs/transport.p12

xpack.security.audit.enabled: true
xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.include:
  - access_denied
  - authentication_failed
  - connection_denied
  - tampered_request
  - access_granted

xpack.security.audit.logfile.events.emit_request_body: false

# S3 repository plugin settings (credentials loaded from keystore, not plaintext)
# s3.client.default.endpoint is configured via elasticsearch-keystore

---
# 3. Snapshot Repository Registration - S3
# File: config/snapshot-repo-s3.yml
# Applied via API call: PUT _snapshot/s3_backup_repo

snapshot_repository:
  name: s3_backup_repo
  type: s3
  settings:
    bucket: elasticsearch-backups-prod
    region: us-east-1
    base_path: snapshots
    server_side_encryption: true
    storage_class: standard_ia
    compress: true
    chunk_size: 1gb
    max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
    max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
    # Credentials are stored in Elasticsearch keystore, NOT in config files
    # Use: bin/elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.access_key
    # Use: bin/elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.secret_key

---
# 4. Snapshot Repository Registration - File Share
# File: config/snapshot-repo-fs.yml
# Applied via API call: PUT _snapshot/fs_backup_repo

snapshot_repository:
  name: fs_backup_repo
  type: fs
  settings:
    location: /usr/share/elasticsearch/backups/snapshots
    compress: true
    chunk_size: 1gb
    max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 200mb
    max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 100mb
    readonly: false

---
# 5. Snapshot Lifecycle Management (SLM) Policies
# File: config/slm-policies.yml

slm_policies:
  # Daily snapshot policy
  - name: daily-snapshots
    schedule: "0 30 2 * * ?"  # 2:30 AM daily
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        taken_by: slm-automation
        policy: daily
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 30d
      min_count: 7
      max_count: 30

  # Hourly snapshot policy for critical indices
  - name: hourly-critical-snapshots
    schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"  # Every hour
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "production-*"
        - "transactions-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      partial: false
      metadata:
        taken_by: slm-automation
        policy: hourly-critical
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 7d
      min_count: 24
      max_count: 168

  # Weekly full snapshot policy
  - name: weekly-full-snapshots
    schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"  # 3:00 AM every Sunday
    repository: s3_backup_repo
    config:
      indices:
        - "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        taken_by: slm-automation
        policy: weekly-full
        environment: production
    retention:
      expire_after: 90d
      min_count: 4
      max_count: 12

---
# 6. Snapshot creation and restoration scripts
# File: config/snapshot-operations.yml

snapshot_operations:
  # Manual snapshot creation template
  create_snapshot:
    endpoint: "PUT _snapshot/{repository}/{snapshot_name}"
    parameters:
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      snapshot_name: "manual-snapshot-{timestamp}"
    body:
      indices: "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: true
      partial: false
      metadata:
        taken_by: operations-team
        reason: manual-backup
        environment: production

  # Restore snapshot template
  restore_snapshot:
    endpoint: "POST _snapshot/{repository}/{snapshot_name}/_restore"
    parameters:
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      snapshot_name: "<snapshot-to-restore>"
    body:
      indices: "*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      rename_pattern: "(.+)"
      rename_replacement: "restored-$1"
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0
      ignore_index_settings:
        - "index.refresh_interval"

  # Restore specific indices
  restore_specific_indices:
    endpoint: "POST _snapshot/{repository}/{snapshot_name}/_restore"
    parameters:
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      snapshot_name: "<snapshot-to-restore>"
    body:
      indices:
        - "critical-*"
        - "production-*"
      ignore_unavailable: true
      include_global_state: false
      rename_pattern: "(.+)"
      rename_replacement: "restored-$1"
      index_settings:
        index.number_of_replicas: 0

  # Check snapshot status
  check_status:
    endpoint: "GET _snapshot/{repository}/{snapshot_name}/_status"

  # List all snapshots
  list_snapshots:
    endpoint: "GET _snapshot/{repository}/_all"

  # Delete snapshot
  delete_snapshot:
    endpoint: "DELETE _snapshot/{repository}/{snapshot_name}"

---
# 7. Security roles for snapshot operations
# File: config/security-roles.yml

security_roles:
  # Snapshot admin role - full snapshot management
  snapshot_admin:
    cluster:
      - manage_slm
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/create
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/delete
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/get
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/restore
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/status
      - cluster:admin/repository/put
      - cluster:admin/repository/delete
      - cluster:admin/repository/get
      - cluster:admin/repository/verify
    indices:
      - names:
          - "*"
        privileges:
          - monitor
          - read
          - view_index_metadata

  # Snapshot viewer role - read-only access
  snapshot_viewer:
    cluster:
      - monitor_snapshot
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/get
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/status
      - cluster:admin/repository/get
    indices:
      - names:
          - "*"
        privileges:
          - monitor
          - view_index_metadata

  # Snapshot restore role - can restore but not create/delete repos
  snapshot_restore_operator:
    cluster:
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/get
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/status
      - cluster:admin/snapshot/restore
      - cluster:admin/repository/get
    indices:
      - names:
          - "restored-*"
        privileges:
          - all
      - names:
          - "*"
        privileges:
          - monitor
          - view_index_metadata

  # Role mappings
  role_mappings:
    - role: snapshot_admin
      users:
        - backup_admin
      realms:
        - native
        - ldap

    - role: snapshot_viewer
      users:
        - ops_viewer
      realms:
        - native
        - ldap

    - role: snapshot_restore_operator
      users:
        - restore_operator
      realms:
        - native
        - ldap

---
# 8. Automation setup script
# File: scripts/setup-snapshot-automation.sh
# This is referenced as a shell script but documented in YAML for clarity

setup_steps:
  description: "Steps to initialize the snapshot infrastructure"

  step_1_install_s3_plugin:
    command: "bin/elasticsearch-plugin install repository-s3"
    note: "Must be done before starting Elasticsearch"

  step_2_add_s3_credentials_to_keystore:
    commands:
      - "bin/elasticsearch-keystore create"
      - "bin/elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.access_key"
      - "bin/elasticsearch-keystore add s3.client.default.secret_key"
    note: "Credentials are entered interactively, never stored in plaintext config"

  step_3_register_s3_repository:
    method: PUT
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_snapshot/s3_backup_repo"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"
    note: "Password provided via prompt or environment variable from secrets manager"
    body:
      type: s3
      settings:
        bucket: elasticsearch-backups-prod
        region: us-east-1
        base_path: snapshots
        server_side_encryption: true
        compress: true

  step_4_register_fs_repository:
    method: PUT
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_snapshot/fs_backup_repo"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"
    body:
      type: fs
      settings:
        location: /usr/share/elasticsearch/backups/snapshots
        compress: true

  step_5_verify_repository:
    method: POST
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_snapshot/s3_backup_repo/_verify"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"

  step_6_create_slm_daily_policy:
    method: PUT
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_slm/policy/daily-snapshots"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"
    body:
      schedule: "0 30 2 * * ?"
      name: "<daily-snap-{now/d}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
      retention:
        expire_after: 30d
        min_count: 7
        max_count: 30

  step_7_create_slm_hourly_critical_policy:
    method: PUT
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_slm/policy/hourly-critical-snapshots"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 * * * ?"
      name: "<hourly-critical-{now/d{yyyy.MM.dd-HH}}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "critical-*"
          - "production-*"
          - "transactions-*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: false
      retention:
        expire_after: 7d
        min_count: 24
        max_count: 168

  step_8_create_slm_weekly_policy:
    method: PUT
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_slm/policy/weekly-full-snapshots"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"
    body:
      schedule: "0 0 3 ? * SUN"
      name: "<weekly-full-{now/d}>"
      repository: s3_backup_repo
      config:
        indices:
          - "*"
        ignore_unavailable: true
        include_global_state: true
      retention:
        expire_after: 90d
        min_count: 4
        max_count: 12

  step_9_start_slm:
    method: POST
    url: "https://localhost:9200/_slm/start"
    auth: "--cacert ca.crt -u backup_admin"

---
# 9. Monitoring and alerting configuration
# File: config/snapshot-monitoring.yml

monitoring:
  # Watcher alert for failed snapshots
  snapshot_failure_alert:
    trigger:
      schedule:
        interval: 15m
    input:
      http:
        request:
          scheme: https
          host: localhost
          port: 9200
          path: "/_slm/stats"
          auth:
            basic:
              # Use watcher keystore for credentials
              username: "{{ctx.metadata.monitor_user}}"
              password: "{{ctx.metadata.monitor_pass_ref}}"
    condition:
      compare:
        ctx.payload.stats.snapshots_failed:
          gt: 0
    actions:
      notify_ops:
        webhook:
          scheme: https
          host: alerts.internal.company.com
          port: 443
          method: post
          path: /api/v1/alerts
          headers:
            Content-Type: application/json
            Authorization: "Bearer {{ctx.metadata.alert_token_ref}}"
          body: |
            {
              "severity": "critical",
              "title": "Elasticsearch Snapshot Failure",
              "message": "Snapshot failures detected: {{ctx.payload.stats.snapshots_failed}} failed snapshots",
              "source": "elasticsearch-backup-monitor",
              "timestamp": "{{ctx.execution_time}}"
            }

  # Health check for snapshot repository
  repository_health_check:
    trigger:
      schedule:
        interval: 1h
    input:
      http:
        request:
          scheme: https
          host: localhost
          port: 9200
          path: "/_snapshot/s3_backup_repo/_verify"
          method: post
          auth:
            basic:
              username: "{{ctx.metadata.monitor_user}}"
              password: "{{ctx.metadata.monitor_pass_ref}}"
    condition:
      compare:
        ctx.payload.status:
          not_eq: 200
    actions:
      notify_ops:
        webhook:
          scheme: https
          host: alerts.internal.company.com
          port: 443
          method: post
          path: /api/v1/alerts
          headers:
            Content-Type: application/json
            Authorization: "Bearer {{ctx.metadata.alert_token_ref}}"
          body: |
            {
              "severity": "critical",
              "title": "Snapshot Repository Health Check Failed",
              "message": "S3 backup repository verification failed",
              "source": "elasticsearch-backup-monitor",
              "timestamp": "{{ctx.execution_time}}"
            }

---
# 10. Disaster Recovery Runbook
# File: config/disaster-recovery-runbook.yml

disaster_recovery:
  description: "Runbook for snapshot-based disaster recovery"

  pre_restore_checklist:
    - "Verify the target cluster is healthy: GET _cluster/health"
    - "Identify the correct snapshot to restore: GET _snapshot/s3_backup_repo/_all"
    - "Check snapshot integrity: GET _snapshot/s3_backup_repo/{snapshot}/_status"
    - "Ensure sufficient disk space on target cluster"
    - "Notify stakeholders of planned restoration"
    - "Close conflicting indices if restoring to same cluster"

  restore_procedures:
    full_cluster_restore:
      steps:
        - description: "Close all indices"
          api: "POST */_close"
        - description: "Restore from snapshot"
          api: "POST _snapshot/s3_backup_repo/{snapshot}/_restore"
          body:
            indices: "*"
            include_global_state: true
        - description: "Monitor restore progress"
          api: "GET _cat/recovery?v&active_only=true"
        - description: "Verify cluster health"
          api: "GET _cluster/health?wait_for_status=green&timeout=5m"

    partial_index_restore:
      steps:
        - description: "Restore specific indices with rename"
          api: "POST _snapshot/s3_backup_repo/{snapshot}/_restore"
          body:
            indices:
              - "target-index-*"
            ignore_unavailable: true
            include_global_state: false
            rename_pattern: "(.+)"
            rename_replacement: "restored-$1"
            index_settings:
              index.number_of_replicas: 0
        - description: "Verify restored indices"
          api: "GET restored-*/_count"
        - description: "Reindex from restored to original if needed"
          api: "POST _reindex"
          body:
            source:
              index: "restored-target-index-*"
            dest:
              index: "target-index-reindexed"

  post_restore_checklist:
    - "Verify all expected indices are present: GET _cat/indices?v"
    - "Check document counts match expectations"
    - "Verify cluster health is green: GET _cluster/health"
    - "Update replica counts if needed: PUT */_settings with number_of_replicas"
    - "Run application-level data integrity checks"
    - "Clean up restored-* temporary indices if used"
    - "Document the restoration in incident log"
